Cubit.summary: A common biblical unit of length based on the forearm.
Cubit.note: The long or royal cubit could be somewhat greater.

Span.summary: A short measure of length based on the spread of the hand.

Handbreadth.summary: A very small unit of length based on the breadth of the hand.
Handbreadth.approximate: About 3 to 4 inches or 7.5 to 10 centimeters.

Reed.summary: A measuring reed used for larger distances.
Reed.approximate: Often about 6 cubits, or roughly 9 feet / 2.7 meters.

Measure.summary: A general English rendering for a quantity, often context-dependent.
Measure.note: The exact modern equivalent depends on the underlying Hebrew or Greek unit.

Omer.summary: A dry measure used in the Old Testament.

Ephah.summary: A larger dry measure used for grain and similar goods.

Bath.summary: A liquid measure used in the Old Testament.

Hin.approximate: About 3.5 to 4 liters, close to 1 gallon.

Log.approximate: About 0.3 liters, or a little over half a pint.

Firkin.approximate: Roughly 8 to 10 gallons, depending on the standard used.

Cor.summary: A large dry or liquid measure, related to the homer.
Cor.note: Often treated as equivalent to a homer.

Homer.summary: A very large measure for dry or liquid goods.

Shekel.summary: A standard biblical unit of weight and also a monetary weight.

Talent.summary: A very large unit of weight, often for precious metals.
Talent.approximate: Often estimated around 75 pounds or 34 kilograms.

Pound.summary: A Roman pound used as a weight and money-weight.

Penny.summary: The KJV rendering of the Roman denarius.
Penny.approximate: Commonly viewed as a laborer's wage for a day.

Farthing.summary: A very small coin in the New Testament.
Farthing.approximate: A tiny fraction of a denarius.

Mite.summary: A very small coin, famously mentioned in the widow's offering.
Mite.approximate: Smaller than a farthing; among the least-value coins mentioned.

Watch.summary: A division of the night for keeping guard and marking time.
Watch.approximate: Usually around 3 hours in the Roman system.

Hour.summary: A standard time division used in Scripture.
